Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 119 upvotes, 10 comments
  2. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 27 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 266 upvotes, 47 comments
  4. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 75 upvotes, 11 comments
  5. About the Atmosphere 47 upvotes, 6 comments
  6. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 237 upvotes, 65 comments
  7. I'll buy your electronics to feed our robot 78 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 130 upvotes, 33 comments
  9. Interview: Nobonoko, Master of the Minimal Sequencer 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. HD Audio Driver for Windows 98SE / Me 18 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 474 upvotes, 183 comments
  12. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 179 upvotes, 63 comments
  13. More on Version Control 66 upvotes, 21 comments
  14. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 537 upvotes, 242 comments
  15. Moretti replication published in AER 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. The Cognitive Dark Forest 382 upvotes, 176 comments
  17. Ohm's Peg-to-WASM Compiler 48 upvotes, 16 comments
  18. New Apple Silicon M4 and M5 HiDPI Limitation on 4K External Displays 222 upvotes, 104 comments
  19. The road signs that teach travellers about France 81 upvotes, 33 comments
  20. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 692 upvotes, 396 comments
  21. Neovim 0.12.0 342 upvotes, 191 comments
  22. "Roadrunner": a bipedal, wheeled robot for multi-modal locomotion [video] 20 upvotes, 6 comments
  23. VHDL's Crown Jewel 17 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 487 upvotes, 338 comments
  25. Hardware Image Compression 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  26. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 202 upvotes, 162 comments
  28. Coding Agents Could Make Free Software Matter Again 146 upvotes, 126 comments
  29. Gonon: Building a Clock with No Numerals 15 upvotes, 10 comments
  30. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 134 upvotes, 159 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 30 March 2026. Source here.
